title: Paws and Celebrate Dog Biscuit Day!
story: Dogs across the country get extra tail-wagging treats on February 23 for National Dog Biscuit Day! Pet owners celebrate by buying special gourmet biscuits, baking homemade treats, or letting their furry friends pick out new flavors at pet stores. Many dog bakeries offer free samples and host "yappy hours" where pups can socialize while munching on bone-shaped goodies. Social media fills with photos of dogs showing off their favorite biscuits or making funny faces while crunching away. Some people even throw dog parties complete with biscuit cakes and party hats. It's the perfect day to spoil your four-legged best friend with something delicious!
activity_question: What do many dog bakeries offer on National Dog Biscuit Day?
bonus_fact: The first dog biscuits were invented in the 1860s by an American electrician!

title: Going Bananas for Banana Bread Day!
story: Every February 23, kitchens across America fill with the sweet smell of banana bread baking in ovens! National Banana Bread Day celebrates this beloved treat that turns overripe bananas into something magical. People share their favorite family recipes, post photos of golden loaves on social media, and gather friends for banana bread swaps. Bakeries often feature special flavors like chocolate chip or walnut banana bread. It's the perfect day to rescue those brown bananas sitting on your counter and transform them into delicious homemade goodness!
activity_question: What do people often do with overripe bananas on National Banana Bread Day?
bonus_fact: Banana bread became popular during the Great Depression when people didn't want to waste food.

title: Serve Up Some Fun on Tennis Day!
story: Tennis rackets are swinging everywhere on February 23 for National Play Tennis Day! People of all ages grab their rackets and head to courts, parks, and even their driveways to hit some balls. Tennis clubs host special events and free lessons for beginners. Many families play fun games like mini-tennis in their backyards or living rooms using balloons instead of real tennis balls. It's the perfect day to learn new serves, practice your backhand, or just have a blast running around the court!
activity_question: What do some families use instead of real tennis balls when playing at home?
follow_up_question: What creative ways could you play tennis without a real court?

title: Sweep Your Way to Cool!
story: Players slide heavy stones across ice while teammates frantically sweep the path with special brooms on Curling Is Cool Day every February 23! This winter sport looks like shuffleboard meets housework, but it's way more exciting. Teams shout instructions as they guide their stones toward colorful targets painted on the ice. The sweeping actually helps the stone travel farther and straighter by melting tiny bits of ice. People celebrate by trying curling at local rinks, watching tournaments, or even making up their own sliding games at home.
activity_question: What do curling players use to help guide their stones?
bonus_fact: Curling stones can weigh up to 44 pounds each!

title: Diesel Engine Day: Powering Up the Fun!
story: Trucks, buses, and trains all rumble to life thanks to diesel engines! On February 23, people celebrate Diesel Engine Day by appreciating these powerful machines that move our world. Car enthusiasts share photos of diesel vehicles online, while truckers honk their horns in celebration. Some museums host special exhibits about how diesel engines work and why they're so important for transportation and industry.
activity_question: What do truckers do to celebrate Diesel Engine Day?
bonus_fact: Diesel engines are about 40% more fuel-efficient than regular gas engines!
